statements: A decrease in aggregate demand leads to a decrease in real GDP.
An increase in the money supply causes a decrease in interest rates.
A rise in inflation expectations leads to an increase in nominal wages.
A decrease in the price of a good causes an increase in the quantity demanded of that good.
An increase in the cost of production leads to a decrease in the supply of a good.
A decrease in the price of a substitute good causes a decrease in the demand for the original good.
A rational player's belief about an opponent's strategy causes the player to choose their own strategy.
The presence of a dominant strategy leads a player to choose that strategy regardless of the opponent's actions.
The ability to commit to a strategy causes a player to gain a strategic advantage.
A decrease in interest rates causes an increase in investment spending.
An increase in stock prices leads to a rise in consumer confidence.
A reduction in corporate taxes causes an increase in after-tax profits.
A reduction in tariffs leads to increased trade volumes.
An appreciation of a country's currency causes a decrease in its exports.
Increased trade openness leads to greater specialization in production.
A strong brand identity causes increased customer loyalty.
Effective market segmentation leads to targeted marketing strategies.
High perceived value results in higher pricing power.
A decrease in interest rates causes an increase in stock prices.
A rise in corporate profits leads to an increase in stock prices.
An increase in investor sentiment causes an increase in stock prices.
A higher risk tolerance leads to a higher allocation to equities.
A decrease in interest rates causes an increase in bond prices.
A rise in inflation expectations leads to a decrease in bond prices.
A high level of speculation in cryptocurrency markets leads to increased price volatility.
Increased adoption of cryptocurrencies by merchants causes a rise in their transactional value.
Regulatory uncertainty causes reduced investment in cryptocurrency markets.
A rise in interest rates causes bond prices to fall.
An increase in inflation expectations leads to a decrease in bond prices.
A reduction in the credit rating of a bond issuer causes the yield on that issuer's bonds to rise.
A decrease in the money supply causes a decrease in aggregate demand.
An increase in interest rates leads to a decrease in investment spending.
A reduction in the discount rate causes an increase in bank reserves.
A decrease in the reserve requirement ratio causes an increase in the money supply.

An increase in non-performing loans causes a reduction in bank capital.
A decrease in government spending leads to a reduction in aggregate demand.
An increase in taxes causes a decrease in disposable income.
Expansionary fiscal policy leads to an increase in interest rates.

- A decrease in the money supply leads to a decrease in inflation.
- An increase in production costs causes an increase in prices.
A decrease in aggregate demand causes an increase in unemployment.
An increase in labor supply leads to an increase in unemployment.
